name: Honda Civic Si (7th Gen EP3)
reliability: 8/10 — the EP3 introduced the K20 to the Si, and the K-series is one of the most durable and swap-friendly engines Honda ever built. The catch is that the US Si got the milder K20A3 (a single-cam-profile i-VTEC that's more economy-minded than the K20A2/Z2 in the RSX Type-S), and the generation switched to a MacPherson-strut front end, so purists rate it below the EM1. None of that hurts reliability — it just makes the EP3 a fantastic, cheap K-swap base. Long underrated, now climbing in value.
cost_to_own: Insurance: Low-moderate · Maintenance ~$400/yr · Still one of the more affordable Si generations, but clean examples are appreciating
popular_mods: A K20A2/K20Z head or a full K-swap — the classic way to unlock the K20A3's potential
RSX Type-S parts (intake manifold, throttle body, cams) to wake up the top end
Coilovers + sway bars to sharpen the strut-front chassis
Header + intake + exhaust with a tune
Honest take: the EP3 is the value-buy K-swap platform — great bones, cheap entry, huge parts support

generations.7th Gen EP3 (2002–2005): The Si became a hatchback with a novel dash-mounted shifter and the 160 hp K20A3 i-VTEC. Polarizing at launch for its shape and mild engine, but beloved now for K-series durability and swap potential.
generations.Note: This generation dropped the front double-wishbone for MacPherson struts — the trade-off enthusiasts most often cite versus the earlier EM1.

common_issues: The K20A3's mild VTEC crossover feels tame stock — the reason so many get head swaps
MacPherson-strut front end is less sharp than the older double-wishbone Sis
VTC actuator cold-start rattle on the K-series — usually benign but worth knowing
Rust on rear arches and rockers in salt climates
Dash-shifter bushings wear and make the shift feel vague
maintenance_tips: The K20 is chain-driven — no timing belt to change, one of its big advantages
Address any VTC actuator rattle if it persists past cold start
Refresh shifter and suspension bushings to tighten the driving feel
Keep the oil fresh — the K-series is happiest on a clean 5,000–7,500 mile interval
If you want power, budget for a head swap or K-swap rather than chasing the K20A3 hard
